# @moku-labs/web — full reference

A content-focused **static-site generator + SPA framework** built on the `@moku-labs/core`
micro-kernel. It SSG-renders Preact pages to static HTML (SEO + first paint), then
progressively enhances them with client-side island hydration and optional DATA-driven
navigation. Browser-safe **isomorphic** plugins are the default composition; **node-only**
plugins are composed à la carte for the build.

- Runtime: **Node 24+**, Bun, Cloudflare Workers, browsers. The route matcher is native `RegExp` (no `URLPattern` global), so matching works in every engine — including Safari/Firefox versions that ship no `URLPattern`.
- Package manager: **bun**. Strict TS: `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`.
- Two entry points: `.` (Node SSG; dual ESM + CJS, full surface) and `./browser` (client bundle; ESM-only, guaranteed node-free). Prefer `./browser` for the client — do NOT rely on tree-shaking `.` for browser safety (see §0).

## 0. Entry points (`.` vs `./browser`)

Two published entries; pick by target:

| Entry | Use | Format | Surface |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `.` | **Node SSG build** | ESM + CJS | full (isomorphic defaults + node-only plugins/providers) |
| `./browser` | **client/browser bundle** | ESM-only | isomorphic surface, node-free, env pre-wired |

- **`.`** — unchanged. Add `contentPlugin`/`buildPlugin`/`deployPlugin`, wire `dotenv`/`processEnv`/`cloudflareBindings` for `env`.
- **`./browser`** — exports the SAME `createApp` / `createPlugin` over the SAME isomorphic default set (`site`, `i18n`, `router`, `head`, `spa` + `log`/`env` core), PLUS `dataPlugin`, `defineRoutes`, `route`, `createUrls`, `createComponent` (spa islands), `browserEnv`, the SEO head primitives (`meta`, `og`, `twitter`, `jsonLd`, `canonical`, `hreflang`, `feedLink`, `buildArticleHead`), and the browser-relevant type namespaces (`Data`, `Env`, `Head`, `Log`, `Router`, `Spa`). It EXCLUDES everything node-only: `contentPlugin`, `buildPlugin`, `deployPlugin`, the node env providers `dotenv`/`processEnv`/`cloudflareBindings`, and the `Build`/`Content`/`Deploy` type namespaces.
- **env is pre-wired:** `./browser` sets `browserEnv()` as the default `env` provider, so env works with ZERO consumer config in the browser — you do NOT pass `pluginConfigs.env.providers`. It resolves from `import.meta.env` and `globalThis.__ENV__`.
- **Guarantee:** importing `@moku-labs/web/browser` can NEVER drag node/native code into a client bundle, regardless of the consumer's bundler or tree-shaking — its static import graph references zero node-only modules. This is STRONGER and more reliable than the previous guidance of "import `@moku-labs/web` and rely on `sideEffects: false` tree-shaking" (which is fragile: building entries together can merge node code into a shared chunk).
- A CI gate (`bun run check:bundle`) asserts the built browser bundle has zero static node/native imports and stays under a gzip size budget (currently ~45 kB gzip, under the 50 kB gz budget).
- `dataPlugin` stays consumer-composed (NOT a default) — compose it for client DATA navigation (`router.mode` `"spa"` | `"hybrid"`); its node write-half is loaded only via dynamic import.

```ts
import { createApp, dataPlugin } from "@moku-labs/web/browser";
import * as routes from "./routes"; // render shells — no node-only content import
// env works with no wiring — browserEnv is the default provider
const app = createApp({ plugins: [dataPlugin], config: { mode: "spa" }, pluginConfigs: { router: { routes } } });
await app.start(); // routes compiled at init (registered via pluginConfigs.router.routes)
```

## 1. The three-layer model (from @moku-labs/core)

1. **Layer 1 — config + events** (`createCoreConfig`): internal.
2. **Layer 2 — framework + plugins** (`createCore`): internal to `@moku-labs/web`.
3. **Layer 3 — consumer**: you call `createApp(...)`. NEVER import `@moku-labs/core`.

`createApp({ plugins?, pluginConfigs })`:
- `plugins` — extra plugin instances ADDED to the isomorphic defaults (e.g. the node-only ones).
- `pluginConfigs` — per-plugin config keyed by plugin name. 4-level cascade (plugin default → framework → consumer), shallow-merged + frozen.
- Returns a frozen `app` exposing `app.start()`, `app.stop()`, and each plugin's API at `app.<name>` (e.g. `app.build`, `app.data`, `app.spa`, `app.content`).

**Defaults (isomorphic):** `site`, `i18n`, `router`, `head`, `spa` + `log`/`env` core.
**Node-only (compose explicitly):** `content`, `build`, `deploy`, `data`, `cli`.

Cross-plugin access is a synchronous PULL via `ctx.require(plugin)` (resolved by name); events
are notification-only (no request/response over events). `ctx.has(name)` checks registration.

## 2. The route is the single contract

```ts
import { route, defineRoutes, contentPlugin } from "@moku-labs/web";

// Routes are plain values; register them via pluginConfigs.router.routes (the sole path).
const routes = defineRoutes({
  home: route("/{lang:?}/")
    .load((ctx) => listCards(ctx.require(contentPlugin), ctx.locale))  // ctx: { params, locale, require, has } → D
    .render((ctx) => <Home cards={ctx.data} url={ctx.url} />)
    .head((ctx) => ({ title: "Home" })),
  post: route("/{lang:?}/{slug}/")
    .generate((ctx) => slugs(ctx.locale).map((slug) => ({ lang: ctx.locale, slug })))  // SSG param sets
    .load((ctx) => ctx.require(contentPlugin).load(ctx.params.slug, ctx.locale))        // sibling API via ctx.require
    .render((ctx) => <Post post={ctx.data} url={ctx.url} />)
    .head((ctx) => ({ title: ctx.data.title }))
    .meta({ section: "blog" })                         // JSON-serializable bag (clientManifest)
});

// Register: pass createApp({ pluginConfigs: { router: { routes } } }) — an `import * as routes`
// namespace works. (The imperative router.set() API was removed; config is the sole path.)
// Links: inside render/head use ctx.url(name, params); outside routes (islands) use createUrls(routes, defaultLocale?).
```

Builder methods (each returns the builder; `.load` widens the data generic `D`):
- `.load((ctx) => D | Promise<D>)` — OPTIONAL data loader. `ctx` is a `LoadContext` `{ params, locale, require, has }`; pull sibling plugin APIs the spec way — `ctx.require(contentPlugin)` (instance-only, no module global). Runs at BUILD only. Omit it for a static page — `ctx.data` is then `unknown`.
- `.render((ctx: { params, data: D, locale, url }) => VNode)` — Preact render. `ctx.url(name, params)` builds links (backed by `router.toUrl`). Runs at build (→ `renderToString`) AND on the client (→ Preact `render`). On a client nav `ctx.data` is the fetched JSON (which the build wrote from `load()`) used directly — no validation step; a malformed/missing file falls back to HTML-over-fetch.
- `.head((ctx) => HeadConfig)` — SEO head.
- `.generate((ctx) => params[])` — OPTIONAL SSG param producer. `ctx` is a `GenerateContext` `{ locale, require, has }`.
- `.meta(record)` — JSON-serializable metadata (shipped in `clientManifest`).

Helpers (pure, app-free — `helpers`, like `route`/`defineRoutes`): `createUrls(routes, defaultLocale?)` → `{ toUrl(name, params) }` builds a route's path by name + params via pattern substitution, with NO running app/router/i18n — import it in components/islands directly (replaces the old `bindRouter` module-global). Pass `defaultLocale` so the builder serves that locale at BARE paths, matching the runtime `router.toUrl` (it is app-free and cannot infer it); omit it to keep every locale prefixed. Inside a route, `.render`/`.head` get the same builder as `ctx.url(name, params)` (no import). A loader reaches the node-only content API via `ctx.require(contentPlugin)` — keep loaders in a node-imported module so `contentPlugin` never enters the client bundle (the `data` plugin bridges page data to the browser).
- `.toJson((ctx) => unknown)` — JSON serializer (e.g. feeds), separate from the data path.
- `.toFile((params) => string)` — output path override.
- `.layout((ctx, children) => VNode)` — persistent chrome wrapper. `ctx` is a `LayoutContext` (render ctx + `meta`), so chrome can read `ctx.locale` / `ctx.meta.activeTab`. Applied in SSG ONLY — on client (spa) navigation the chrome persists and only the inner swap region is replaced.

Pattern syntax: `{name}` required param, `{name:?}` optional, `{lang:?}` is the locale-prefix slot (excluded from specificity). On a `{lang:?}` route the DEFAULT locale is served at BARE paths (see §10); required `{lang}` routes are unchanged.

### RouterApi (`ctx.require(routerPlugin)` / `app.router`)
- `match(pathname) => { params, route } | null` — specificity-sorted; most specific wins.
- `toUrl(name, params) => string` — substitute params; throws on unknown name.
- `entries() => readonly TypedRoute[]` — URL-utility view, specificity order.
- `manifest() => readonly RouteDefinition[]` — full defs with `_handlers` (load/render/head/generate), declaration order (build).
- `clientManifest() => readonly { pattern, name, meta }[]` — serializable projection (no closures).
- `mode() => "ssg" | "spa" | "hybrid"` — the SINGLE source of truth for static/hybrid/spa behavior.

Config: router config is just `{ routes?: RouteMap }` — the declarative route map (compiled in `onInit`), the SOLE registration path (the imperative `router.set()` API was REMOVED — there is no runtime re-registration). The render mode is NOT router config — it is the GLOBAL `config.mode` (`"ssg" | "spa" | "hybrid"`, default `"hybrid"`).

## 3. SSG → DATA → SPA (the data flow)

```
BUILD (router.mode !== "ssg")              ON DISK                      CLIENT NAV
 for each route instance:                  dist/en/post/index.html      click /en/p2/
   data = route.load(ctx)             ───► (SSG HTML: SEO+first paint)  1. router.match(path)
   html = renderToString(render(data)) ──►                              2. data.at(path) → fetch
   app.data.write([{path,data}])      ───► dist/_data/en/post/index.json   /_data/en/p2/index.json
                                            (REAL data, never HTML)      3. ctx.data = fetched JSON
                                                                          4. route.render(ctx)→Preact
                                                                          (miss/throw → HTML fetch → location.href)
```

- Persisted data = `load()`'s output (NOT a divergent projection) → the SAME `render` runs both
  sides → SSR/client parity is structural.
- Granularity follows `load`: a list route loads slim cards (small file); a detail route loads one
  record (per-page file). Fetched on demand — payload is independent of site size.
- `route.load` does NOT run on the client; the build already persisted its output.

### Data provider API (`app.data`) — agnostic, optional, isomorphic
- `write(entries: { path, data }[], options?: { outDir? }) => Promise<{ fileCount, bytes, files }>` — Node; persists one JSON per page (keyed by `fileFor`). Called by `build`; lazy `node:fs` writer (browser-safe).
- `at(path) => Promise<unknown | null>` — browser; fetch+cache the page's JSON (`null` on miss/parse-fail).
- `urlFor(path) => string` — `/en/p/` → `/_data/en/p/index.json` (fetch URL).
- `fileFor(path) => string` — `/en/p/` → `_data/en/p/index.json` (outDir-relative file).
- Config: `data: { outputDir?: "_data", baseUrl?: "/_data/" }`. Knows NO domain types — any Layer-3 shape integrates via its route's `load`/`render`.

## 4. spa — client runtime (island hydration + navigation)

Default plugin, `depends: [router, head]`, inert on Node (`onStart` no-ops without `document`).
On `app.start()` in the browser, it mounts islands onto the SSR'd DOM and intercepts navigation.

- Islands: `createComponent(name, hooks)` with lifecycle `onCreate` / `onMount` / `onNavStart` / `onNavEnd` / `onUnMount` / `onDestroy` (ctx: `{ el, data }`, where `data` is the page payload from `script#__DATA__`). Match elements via `data-component="name"`. Components OUTSIDE the swap region are persistent across navigations.
- `app.spa.register(component)`, `app.spa.navigate(path)`, `app.spa.current()`.
- Config: `spa: { swapSelector?: "main > section", viewTransitions?: false, progressBar?: true, components?: [] }`.
- Navigation strategy: when `router.mode() !== "ssg"` AND `data` is composed → DATA path (match → `data.at` → `route.render`, Preact render lazy-loaded in its own chunk); otherwise HTML-over-fetch; failure → `location.href`. `spa` imports neither `data` nor its types (captures the reader via a structural by-name `ctx.require`).
- Events: `spa:navigate`, `spa:navigated`, `spa:component-mount`, `spa:component-unmount`.
- The browser runtime is just your own `createApp(...).start()` over the defaults (+ `dataPlugin` for DATA nav), imported from the `./browser` entry (see §0). There is no framework `hydrate()` / `./client` export.

## 5. head — SEO `<head>` composition

`head.render(resolvedRoute, data) => string` composes title/meta/canonical/OG/Twitter/JSON-LD/hreflang.
`head.siteHead({ url, locale? }) => string` composes the SITE-LEVEL OG/Twitter block (`og:type=website`, site name/description, absolutized `og:url`/`og:image`, `twitter:card`, `og:locale`) for a page with NO route identity — e.g. the bare-path redirects a `localeRedirects` build emits, which the build injects it into. Returns `""` unless `defaultOgImage` is configured, so opted-out apps keep a bare redirect.
Config: `head: { titleTemplate?: "%s — Site", defaultOgImage?: "/og-default.png" }`. Title/description/baseURL come from `site`.

SEO primitives for `.head()` handlers (all exported):
`meta`, `og`, `twitter`, `jsonLd`, `canonical`, `hreflang`, `feedLink`, `buildArticleHead`.
`HeadConfig = { title?, description?, canonical?, image?, elements?: HeadElement[] }`.

## 6. content — markdown pipeline (node-only)

`app.content`:
- `loadAll() => Promise<Map<locale, Article[]>>` — production-filtered (drafts excluded in prod).
- `load(slug, locale) => Promise<Article>` — single article (NOT draft-filtered; use loadAll for safe sets).
- `renderMarkdown(md) => Promise<string>`, `invalidate(paths)`, `articleToCard(article) => ArticleCard`.
- `Article = { frontmatter, computed: { slug, readingTime, contentId, status, wordCount }, html, locale, isFallback, url }`. `ArticleCard` is the slim list projection (title/date/description/tags/readingTime/url — no body).
- Config: `content: { providers: [fileSystemContent({ contentDir, defaultAuthor?, trustedContent?, shikiTheme? })] }` — the plugin SHELL is browser-safe (orchestration: locale fallback, drafts, sort, cache, events); source I/O + the Markdown pipeline live in the composed provider (mirrors `env` providers). `contentPlugin` is exported from `/browser` (for `ctx.require(contentPlugin)` in build-only loaders); `fileSystemContent` (node) from the package root only. Articles live in `contentDir/<slug>/<locale>.md` with YAML frontmatter (`title`, `date`, `description`, `tags`, `language`, optional `author`, `draft`).

## 7. build — SSG orchestrator (node-only)

`app.build.run(options?: { outDir? }) => Promise<{ outDir, pageCount, durationMs }>`. Phases:
0. **clean** — `rm(outDir)`.
1. **bundle** — optional client JS/CSS (injectable runner).
2. **content + images** (parallel) — `content.loadAll()`; copy static image dirs.
3. **pages** — expand routes ONCE; per instance: `load` → `head.render` → `render` → write `outDir/<path>/index.html`. The default locale is served BARE, so each default-locale page on a `{lang:?}` route is ALSO written to `/{defaultLocale}/…` — a content-identical alias whose canonical points at the bare URL — so an explicit `/{defaultLocale}/…` link resolves directly (no redirect). **When `router.mode() !== "ssg"` and `data` is composed**, the same expansion persists each client-navigable page's data (the `load()` output, or `{}` for a no-`load` static route) via `app.data.write(...)` — so even static pages get a sidecar and hybrid nav resolves cleanly.
4. **feeds + sitemap + og-images** (parallel, gated by config flags).
5. **root-index** — write the default `/` page to `outDir/index.html`.
Emits `build:complete { outDir, pageCount, durationMs }`.
Config flags include: `outDir`, `feeds`, `sitemap`, `images`, `ogImage`, `minify`, `injectAssets`, `publicDir`, `notFound`, `localeRedirects`, `clientEntry`, `template`, `ogImage: { template, fontDir, fontName, fonts, defaultCard, render }`.
- `notFound: true | { body } | { path }` — `outDir/404.html` as the built-in default page | an HTML fragment wrapped in the minimal shell | a COMPLETE HTML page file written VERBATIM (the app owns the whole document: its own `<head>`, asset links, body). `path` wins over `body`; an unreadable file fails the build with a clear error.
- `ogImage.defaultCard: boolean | ((input: RichOgInput) => VNode)` — render a SITE-LEVEL default OG card to `outDir/og-default.png` (same loaded fonts + satori → resvg pipeline as article cards). `true` = the built-in generic site card (name over description); a function = your OWN branded card, still rendered by the framework. The per-article `render` hook is NEVER applied to the site card. Point `head.defaultOgImage` at `"/og-default.png"` to serve it (also lights up `head.siteHead` on bare redirects, see §5). Default `false`.
- `template` — custom document shell with four placeholders: `<!--moku:head-->` (composed `<head>` inner HTML), `<!--moku:body-->` (SSR body), `<!--moku:assets-->` (injected `<link>`/`<script>` tags), `<!--moku:lang-->` (the page locale, so a shared shell stays locale-correct for `<html lang>`).
**Call order:** `await app.build.run(); /* data sidecars written within */`.

## 8. deploy — Cloudflare Pages (node-only)

Scaffolds `wrangler.jsonc` and deploys the built `dist/`. Compose `deployPlugin`; see its README.

## 9. env — validated environment access (core)

Compose a provider per target via `env: { providers: [...] }`:
- Node (`.` entry): `dotenv()`, `processEnv()`, `cloudflareBindings()` — wire explicitly.
- Browser (`./browser` entry): `browserEnv()` (reads `import.meta.env` + `globalThis.__ENV__`) is the PRE-WIRED default — env works with ZERO config, no `providers` array needed. Keeps `node:fs` out of the client bundle.

## 10. site / i18n (defaults)

- `site: { name, url, author, description }` → `app.site` (`url()`, `name()`, `canonical(path)`, …).
- `i18n: { locales, defaultLocale, localeNames?, ogLocaleMap?, translations? }` → `app.i18n` (`locales()`, `defaultLocale()`, …). The `{lang:?}` route slot + locale fallback derive from this.
- **The default locale is served at BARE paths** on routes with an optional `{lang:?}` segment — `/`, `/archive/`, `/post/` — while non-default locales keep their `/{locale}/` prefix. (Required `{lang}` routes are unchanged.) No config flag: it is the default behavior. `toUrl`/`toFile`, canonical, hreflang, x-default, sitemap, and SPA nav all emit the bare URL for the default locale; `content` strips the default-locale prefix from `article.url` so article canonicals + feed GUIDs match; the build also emits `/{defaultLocale}/…` alias pages (see §7 phase 3). The pure `createUrls(routes, defaultLocale?)` takes the default locale to match (see §2).

## 11. Testing (vitest)

- Two projects: `unit` (`tests/unit/**`, `src/plugins/**/__tests__/unit/**`) and `integration` (`tests/integration/**`, `src/plugins/**/__tests__/integration/**`). Browser-side tests use `// @vitest-environment happy-dom`.
- Coverage thresholds 85% (istanbul). Framework-level integration drives the REAL `createApp`.
- Plugin tests are colocated; root `tests/` is framework-level only.

## 12. Key invariants

- Defaults are browser-safe; node-only plugins are opt-in. For the client, import the `./browser` entry — it is guaranteed node-free by construction (zero static node imports), not by relying on the consumer's tree-shaking (see §0).
- The route owns rendering; the `data` provider owns transport (page path → JSON). No HTML-in-JSON — the persisted JSON (the build's `load()` output) IS the page payload, used directly as `ctx.data`.
- `node:*` and Preact DOM `render` are isolated behind lazy `import()` (split chunks) so a data-composing browser bundle stays node-free and a no-data bundle ships no render layer.
- Single mode (`router.mode`); no parallel runtime / hand-rolled `boot()`; everything flows through the factory chain.

## Per-plugin READMEs
site · i18n · router · head · spa · content · build · deploy · data · cli · log · env — see `src/plugins/<name>/README.md`.
